status: real TESS injection/recovery calibration; not a discovery search
interpretation: A duration-dependent sensitivity boundary is now directly measured on three real bright-star TESS products. 100 ppm is high-completeness overall but not uniformly reliable across targets; 200 ppm is near-saturated on this sample.
control_metric_caveat: The v0.6 SNR-above-control value subtracts the single strongest brightening across all searched durations. It is deliberately conservative but duration-mismatched; the next engine version will use duration-matched empirical control distributions.
caveats: Box-shaped injections are not yet limb-darkened transit models.
Three bright stars do not represent the full TESS population.
Recovery means detection near the injected time, not astrophysical validation.
The result measures one downloaded product per target.
